learning_outcomes.description: Apply crop physiology frameworks in the design and interpretation of field crop trials.
Predict how crop species physiology impacts on their adaptation and yield as limited by the physical environments of soil, climate (including frost, heat and drought) and nitrogen management.
Use a farming systems model (APSIM) to run simulations that vary for agronomic or genetic factors and interpret the results of these simulations.
Optimise management for crop yield and quality using crop phenology incorporating species, genotype, location and time of sowing.

Analyse how resource capture (canopy leaf area and root systems) and carbon and nitrogen partitioning vary with species, genotype and management factors and how these dynamics affect crop growth and yield.

Apply a working knowledge of how physiological instrumentation and analytical methods based on multiple technologies (satellite imagery, UAVs, static sensors, machine learning, artificial intelligence) can be used in crop research and production.

assessments.weighting: <dd>25% (Each task contributes 5% towards the sequential practical assessment task total of 25%) </dd>
assessments.due_date:
1: APSIM 1 (5%) 3/08/2026 - 11/08/2026

2: APSIM 2 (5%) 11/08/2026 - 17/08/2026

3: RUE Quiz (5%) 24/08/2026 - 31/08/2026

4: TE Quiz (5%) 7/09/2026 - 14/09/2026

5: APSIM 3 (5%) 12/10/2026 - 19/10/2026

(These open at 12pm on the day of the practical and must be submitted by 11am the following week as per date range).
assessments.task_description:
Practical Assessment tasks are quizzes and assess Learning Objectives 1, 2, 3-5. 

  • Assessment 1: APSIM 1 Introduction (5%): The exercise tests knowledge gained while learning to use APSIM to understand how models work and how to interpret their outputs (week 2). The exercise opens in blackboard on the day of the practical and students have until the due date time to finish the exercise. This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 3, 4 & 5. 
  • Assessment 2: APSIM 2 Flowering (5%): The exercise tests knowledge gained while learning to use APSIM to understand how models work and how to interpret their outputs (week 2, 3). The exercise opens in blackboard on the day of the practical and students have until the due date time to finish the exercise. This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 3, 4 & 5. 
  • Assessment 3: RUE (5%): The exercise tests knowledge gained while learning to measure Radiation Use Efficiency (weeks 4, 5). The exercise opens in blackboard on the day of the practical and students have until the due date time to finish the exercise. This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 1, 2, & 5. 
  • Assessment 4: TE (5%): The exercise tests knowledge gained while learning to measure Transpiration Efficiency (weeks 7). The exercise opens in blackboard on the day of the practical and students have until the due date time to finish the exercise. This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 1, 2, & 5.
  • Assessment 5: APSIM 3 GxExM (5%): The exercise tests knowledge gained while learning to use APSIM to understand how models work and how to interpret their outputs (weeks 2, 3 and 11). The exercise opens in blackboard on the day of the practical and students have until the due date time to finish the exercise. This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 3, 4, & 5. 


Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. 

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. 

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessment 1-5 Computer-based assessments must be accessed and completed via Blackboard. These must be completed within the specified time and submitted by 11am in the following week as per last date in the date range for each.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Report 1: This is a report on the measurement and calculation of leaf area, light extinction coefficient and crop growth rate from field trials. It will use data collected from field trials (or existing data from previous experiments when necessary). During workshops students will process data from the field measurements from different experiments and will need to be able to calculate biomass and crop growth rate from field samples. After processing, students will need to summarise data and independently write reports on the results, including tables and graphs of results and a discussion of treatments responses. A detailed task description will be provided on the blackboard site and discussed in the tutorials (in practical session, recorded, zoom). This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 1, 2, 4, 5 & 6. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. 

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. 

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students must submit their assessment task individually, in Blackboard, via the assessment's Turnitin link by the submission deadline. You should also retain an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit. When marks and feedback are available for this assessment item you will be notified via a blackboard announcement.

assessments.task_description:
Report 2: This report is based on field collected data with a focus on yield, grain number and grain size. This scientific report will include topic background, data analysis, results presentation including tables and graphs and a thorough interpretation using the scientific literature. A detailed task description will be provided on the blackboard site and discussed in the tutorials (in practical session, recorded, zoom). This assessment task will assess Learning Objectives 1, 2, 4, 5 & 6. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. 

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
Closed book, two-hour written exam covering all aspects of the course.

The End of Semester Exam will comprise short and extended answer questions as well as solving problems (calculations around estimation of crop growth). The answers to the exam questions are based on the content in this course, including lectures, readings and practicals.

The exam will be an on-campus exam. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams. Students enrolled in In Person delivery must attend the exam in-person.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
